role: Founding Director, Iran Program, Middle East Institute
bio: Alex Vatanka is the founding director of the Iran Program at the [Middle East Institute](/orgs/middle-east-institute) in Washington. A specialist on Iranian foreign policy, IRGC networks, and Iran-Pakistan relations, he has tracked Iranian security and political dynamics for over two decades. He is the author of *Iran and Pakistan: Security, Diplomacy and American Influence* (2015) and *The Battle of the Ayatollahs in Iran: The United States, Foreign Policy and Political Rivalry since 1979* (2021). Vatanka is a frequent analyst in Western media and a regular congressional briefer on Iranian affairs.
editorial_notes: Founding director of MEI Iran Program. Author of two books on Iran. Born year not publicly confirmed.
